note: Submodule of search_api_exclude_entity. Ships one Search API processor that excludes items when an indexed field matches a configured value. Cases are `recipe` probing documented facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal Search API index I want to exclude items whose existing indexed field equals a certain value, without adding a new field. Which processor and which module do I use?
evals.expected_output: Enable the Search API Exclude Entity By Field module and its processor 'search_api_exclude_entity_by_field_processor' on the index.

evals.prompt: How do I tell the Search API Exclude Entity By Field processor which value should exclude an item?
evals.expected_output: In the processor settings there is one textfield per index field; enter the value that should trigger exclusion for that field. Blank fields are ignored.

evals.prompt: In Search API Exclude Entity By Field, how do I match a boolean field that should be excluded when it is false?
evals.expected_output: Enter the literal string 'false' — sanitizeFilterString() converts 'true'/'false' to the boolean TRUE/FALSE before comparing; other strings compare literally.

evals.prompt: What does the Search API Exclude Entity By Field submodule depend on?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the parent module search_api_exclude_entity (which in turn requires search_api).

evals.prompt: At which Search API processing stage does the Exclude Entity By Field processor drop items?
evals.expected_output: The alter_items stage (weight -50); alterIndexedItems() unsets matching items so they never reach the backend.
